query: Copernicus Climate Pulse daily global surface air temperature anomaly June 2026, what are the daily values for June 1-14 2026?
answer:
The sub-agent was able to retrieve the data directly from the Copernicus/ECMWF ERA5 data file. Here is my full synthesis:

---

## Copernicus Climate Pulse: Daily Global Surface Air Temperature Anomaly — June 1–14, 2026

### Data Source & Methodology
The Copernicus Climate Pulse dashboard, operated by the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) at ECMWF, provides daily global mean 2-metre surface air temperature derived from ERA5 reanalysis. Anomalies are computed relative to the **1991–2020 daily climatology**. The underlying data file is published at `https://sites.ecmwf.int/data/climatepulse/data/series/era5_daily_series_2t_global.csv`.

### Daily Values: June 1–14, 2026

| Date | Absolute Temp (°C) | 1991–2020 Climatology (°C) | Anomaly (°C) | Status |
|------------|--------------------:|---------------------------:|--------------:|-------------|
| 2026-06-01 | 16.232 | 15.696 | **+0.536** | Final |
| 2026-06-02 | 16.288 | 15.721 | **+0.567** | Final |
| 2026-06-03 | 16.354 | 15.746 | **+0.608** | Final |
| 2026-06-04 | 16.381 | 15.771 | **+0.611** | Final |
| 2026-06-05 | 16.400 | 15.795 | **+0.605** | Final |
| 2026-06-06 | 16.470 | 15.819 | **+0.651** | Final |
| 2026-06-07 | 16.495 | 15.842 | **+0.653** | Final |
| 2026-06-08 | 16.510 | 15.864 | **+0.646** | Final |
| 2026-06-09 | 16.493 | 15.886 | **+0.607** | Final |
| 2026-06-10 | 16.503 | 15.907 | **+0.596** | Final |
| 2026-06-11 | 16.550 | 15.928 | **+0.622** | Final |
| 2026-06-12 | 16.548 | 15.948 | **+0.600** | Final |
| 2026-06-13 | 16.502 | 15.967 | **+0.535** | Final |
| 2026-06-14 | 16.434 | 15.985 | **+0.449** | Preliminary |

### Key Observations

- **All 14 days show positive anomalies** relative to the 1991–2020 baseline, ranging from **+0.449 °C to +0.653 °C**.
- **Peak anomaly**: June 7 at **+0.653 °C** above the 1991–2020 climatology.
- **Lowest anomaly**: June 14 at **+0.449 °C** (still marked preliminary; may be revised).
- **Mid-month decline**: Anomalies peaked around June 6–8 (~+0.65 °C) and then declined toward June 13–14 (~+0.45–0.54 °C).
- **Mean anomaly** for the 14-day period: approximately **+0.592 °C**.
- 13 of 14 days carry **final** status; only June 14 is **preliminary** due to the ERA5 data processing lag (~5 days behind real-time).
- These anomaly levels are notably **lower** than the extreme values seen during the 2023–2024 El Niño period (when daily anomalies frequently exceeded +1.0 °C), consistent with the transition to ENSO-neutral or weak La Niña conditions.

sources.snippet: Interactive dashboard showing daily global surface air temperature anomalies from ERA5 reanalysis relative to 1991-2020 baseline.

key_facts: All 14 days (June 1-14, 2026) show positive global surface air temperature anomalies relative to the 1991-2020 baseline, ranging from +0.449°C to +0.653°C.
The highest anomaly was on June 7 at +0.653°C above the 1991-2020 climatology.
The lowest anomaly was on June 14 at +0.449°C (preliminary value, subject to revision).
Anomalies peaked around June 6-8 (~+0.65°C) and then declined toward June 13-14 (~+0.45-0.54°C).
The mean anomaly for the 14-day period is approximately +0.592°C.
13 of 14 days have final data status; only June 14 is preliminary due to ERA5 processing lag.
These anomaly levels are notably lower than the 2023-2024 El Niño period when daily anomalies frequently exceeded +1.0°C.
Data is derived from ERA5 reanalysis by ECMWF for the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S).
